entries.summary: Team analysis now includes two new sub-analyses that deepen your understanding of how your team works. AI adoption scanning finds markers of Claude, Copilot, Cursor, aider, Devin, Codeium, and other AI tools in your recent commits and pull requests across GitHub, Azure DevOps, and your local git clone — showing you the AI footprint as a lower bound with source-aware reporting and real evidence links. Documentation quality analysis scores the clarity and AI-likelihood of your team's Notion and Confluence pages, again with cited examples and improvement suggestions. Both ride on the existing team profile machinery, gracefully skip when nothing is configured, and frame their findings honestly as footprints and heuristics rather than definitive counts.

highlights.text: New AI adoption sub-analysis: detect Claude, Copilot, Cursor, aider, Devin, Codeium markers in commits and PRs
AI adoption scans GitHub, Azure DevOps, and local git clones with source-aware breakdowns and evidence links
New documentation quality sub-analysis: score Notion/Confluence page clarity and estimated AI likelihood
Both analyses include evidence-linked coaching citing specific commits, PRs, and pages with real links

entries.summary: Retro now carries action items forward from the previous retro to this one, showing them in a dedicated "Last sprint's actions" review column on the browser board and TUI host view. Your team sets a status for each carried item — Done, Pending, In Progress, Carried Over, or Not Relevant — and these statuses persist in the report. Items marked as Carried Over re-enter the current sprint's action grid and keep the team focused on the context that still matters; Done and Not Relevant items drop out. Carry-forward works whether an item was AI-generated or typed in by hand, and now also survives closing and reopening a retro under the same session. The "Share Remotely" button also hardened: it now waits until the Cloudflare tunnel hostname is externally resolvable before advertising the URL, so opening the link doesn't hit an NXDOMAIN that resolvers will cache for 30 minutes.

highlights.text: Carried-forward action items from the previous retro with status tracking (Done/Pending/In Progress/Carried Over/Not Relevant)
Previous retro's items re-enter the current sprint's action grid if marked Carried Over
Hardened Share Remotely: waits for DNS resolution before advertising the Cloudflare tunnel URL

entries.summary: The token setup wizard now shows you exactly where to create each credential and what access to grant it. GitHub PAT, Azure DevOps PAT, and Jira/Notion/Confluence token fields display a clickable creation URL as an OSC-8 terminal hyperlink and show the required scopes underneath — the same scopes that power all your integrations. The Settings page's credential dashboard now shows these links and scopes too, so you can review them any time and quickly re-create or upgrade a token without searching the documentation.

highlights.text: Setup wizard shows clickable token creation URLs and required scopes under each credential field
Settings dashboard displays token creation links and scopes for every configured credential
Scopes accurately reflect what each integration actually uses — GitHub repo access, Azure work-item creation, Jira ticket reads, etc.

entries.summary: You can now share real yeaboi output publicly without leaking anything private. Every mode's result screen — planning, analysis, standups, retros, performance, reporting, and roadmaps — has a new Anonymize button: press it and the sensitive words (people's names, team and project names, your company name, internal tools, and links) get masked right there in the same cards and tables — the layout never changes, only the words do, so it still reads as a realistic example. It uses AI for the smart cases and always masks the obvious company terms it already knows about, even offline. Refine it in plain language with Adjust ("also hide this", "leave that — it's public"), flip back to the real names with Revert, then copy the clean version to your clipboard or export it to Files, Notion, or Confluence. Great for READMEs, your website, and posts.

highlights.text: New Anonymize button on every mode's result screen masks names, teams, projects, tools, and links in place — same cards and tables, only the words change
Adjust what's masked in plain language, Revert to the real names instantly, then copy to clipboard or export to Files/Notion/Confluence
Set ANONYMIZE_MASK_TERMS to always hide your company-specific words, and it works even without an AI provider
New 'Copy to clipboard' option in every Export menu — plus a Copy button on the Usage and Changelog pages — for a quick, zero-setup way to pull data out of the terminal

entries.summary: yeaboi can now be driven from other AI tools, not just its own terminal app. It ships as a Claude Code plugin with guided skills, and every mode - planning, standups, retros, performance reviews, and reporting - is now reachable from the command line as well as the full-screen app, so you can script or automate anything you used to do by hand. The command line also got friendlier: clearer error messages for bad input, and a strict mode that fails loudly instead of quietly degrading, which is handy for automated pipelines.

highlights.text: yeaboi is now available as a Claude Code plugin with guided setup skills
New command-line subcommands bring reporting, standups, performance, retros, and team analysis to scripts and automation, not just the app
Clearer command-line error messages for bad dates, unknown sources, and typos in engineer names
New strict mode for automated runs that fails clearly instead of silently falling back

entries.summary: Several fixes and small additions for local Ollama mode. Switching from a local model back to a cloud AI provider no longer leaves the old local model name lingering in your settings. When you quit the app with a local model server running, you'll now be offered a quick way to stop it or free up the memory it was using. And the Usage page now shows performance details for local models, like response speed and tokens per second, so you can see how your local setup is actually performing.

highlights.text: Fixed the old local model name sticking around after switching to a cloud AI provider
On quit, offer to stop the local Ollama server or free up its memory
Usage page now shows local model performance: response speed and tokens per second

entries.summary: Planning now has a fourth way to start a project: point it at your quarterly roadmap — a Confluence or Notion page, or a local document — and it reads through it to pull out the concrete candidate projects, ranks them by suggested start order, and sizes each as small or large. Pick one and its description is pre-filled into the normal planning flow, so you skip re-typing what's already written down. Saved roadmap analyses show up right alongside your other projects, tagged so you can tell them apart, and can be exported the same way as everything else.

highlights.text: New Roadmap intake: analyze a quarterly roadmap document into ranked, sized candidate projects
Pick a candidate project to pre-fill its description into the normal planning intake
Saved roadmap analyses appear alongside your projects, tagged and exportable to Files, Notion, or Confluence
Improved reliability for newer AI models used in planning and every ceremony mode

entries.summary: You can now run yeaboi completely for free with no API key by using a local Ollama model instead of a cloud AI provider — planning, standups, retros, performance reviews, reporting, and chat all work fully offline with nothing leaving your machine. The setup wizard walks you through picking a downloaded model (or downloading one right from the app, with live progress) and explains the size/speed/RAM trade-offs of each. Extra work under the hood keeps local models just as reliable as cloud ones, with clear on-screen guidance if the local server isn't running or a model needs to be downloaded first.

highlights.text: New free local mode: run yeaboi entirely offline with Ollama, no API key required
Setup wizard adds an Ollama option with live model discovery and per-model size/speed/RAM guidance
Download and verify local models directly from the app, with live progress and resumable downloads
Clearer, more reliable output from local models across planning and all ceremony modes
Usage page shows $0 cost for local runs while still tracking your past cloud spend

entries.summary: Fixed a bug where keys you pressed while the terminal UI was busy rendering a frame could be silently dropped instead of registering — now every keypress is reliably picked up, even ones sent while the app is mid-render.

highlights.text: Fixed dropped keypresses in the terminal UI when input arrived while a frame was rendering

entries.summary: You can now send bug reports and feature requests straight from the app instead of leaving to file a GitHub issue. Press f from the mode select screen to open a quick feedback form, pick a type and area, write a title and description (with voice dictation and screenshot paste support), and optionally let AI polish your wording before you submit. Your feedback is filed as a GitHub issue automatically, or opens a pre-filled page in your browser if that's not set up.

highlights.text: New Feedback page (press f) for filing bug reports and feature requests without leaving the app
Feedback form supports voice dictation and screenshot paste, same as other text fields
Optional AI Polish rewrites your draft with repro steps or user-story framing before you submit
Submissions file a GitHub issue automatically, or open a pre-filled issue page in your browser

entries.summary: Analysis mode now gives you actionable coaching on how to improve your team before it moves on to suggesting sample tickets. After you see your results, a new Team Insights page shows four categories - things to start doing, stop doing, keep doing, and things worth trying - covering team setup, ways of working, estimation, process, and work mix. Every suggestion comes with practical advice and points to the specific stat from your analysis that triggered it, and you can export the page just like any other report.

highlights.text: New Team Insights page after analysis results: start/stop/keep doing plus worth-trying suggestions
Each insight includes practical advice and cites the specific stat that triggered it
Team Insights is exportable to Files, Notion, or Confluence like other analysis sections

entries.summary: Every Export button across the app now opens a single destination picker so you can send your work straight to Files, Notion, or Confluence, with only the destinations you've actually set up shown. Notion and Confluence pages are no longer plain pasted markdown — they come out as properly formatted native pages with tables, task lists, callouts, and embedded charts, all branded with a yeaboi icon and footer, and grouped together under an auto-created yeaboi page if you haven't picked a specific spot for them. Team analysis and delivery reports can now include sprint velocity and delivered-work charts as images. There's also a single setting to change where yeaboi stores everything on your computer.

highlights.text: Every Export button now opens one picker for Files, Notion, or Confluence, showing only configured destinations
Notion and Confluence exports are natively formatted with tables, task lists, and callouts instead of raw markdown
Exported pages carry yeaboi branding and group under an auto-created yeaboi page by default
Team analysis and delivery reports can embed sprint velocity and delivered-work charts
New single setting to change where yeaboi stores its data on your computer

entries.summary: The standup roster is cleaner and the standup page itself is redesigned into a compact dashboard. Bot accounts and people who only commented on or edited a ticket no longer show up as team members — only people actually assigned work do. The standup view now leads with a pinned status strip showing sprint day, progress, and confidence at a glance, team member rows are easier to scan with clear active/quiet/self-reported markers, and duplicated information has been cleared out so more fits on screen at once.

highlights.text: Bot accounts and drive-by commenters are filtered out of the standup team roster — only assignees count
Standup redesigned into a compact dashboard with a pinned sprint day / progress / confidence strip
Team member rows now show clear active/quiet/self-reported activity markers
Removed duplicated summary text and rows so the standup view fits more on screen

entries.summary: Daily Standup now sees a lot more of what your team actually did — status changes, comments, and in-progress tickets all count, not just who was assigned a task, so quiet team members get a real update instead of "No activity detected." When a source like GitHub or Confluence isn't connected, the report now tells you plainly instead of just going quiet. The standup overview is also decluttered: your own update gets its own row, and the rest of the team collapses into a single expandable row.

highlights.text: Standup now credits status changes, comments, and in-progress tickets across Jira, Azure DevOps, Confluence, and GitHub, not just assignments
Reports now call out which sources weren't scanned and why, so you know what to connect
Overview redesigned: your update is its own row, teammates collapse into one expandable Team row

entries.summary: You can now paste a screenshot straight from your clipboard into any text box with Ctrl+V, just like Claude Code. A small chip shows where the image landed in your text, and it travels with your message so the AI can see and reason about what you pasted — in project descriptions, feedback when regenerating, chat, standup updates, and 1:1 transcripts.

highlights.text: Paste screenshots into text fields with Ctrl+V; a chip marks where the image is attached
Pasted images are sent to the AI in project descriptions, regenerate feedback, and chat
Pasted images also supported in Daily Standup updates and Performance 1:1 transcripts

entries.summary: yeaboi now keeps you up to date and tells you what's new. The welcome screen shows the version you're running in the bottom-left corner and quietly checks PyPI in the background — when a newer release exists, it shows the exact upgrade command for how you installed. Press c to open the new Changelog page: an AI-written summary of every release, with each change tagged by the feature it touches in that feature's colour.

highlights.text: Version indicator with automatic update check and installer-aware upgrade hint
In-app Changelog page (press c on the welcome screen) with colour-coded feature tags
Releases now ship their changelog entry automatically alongside each version bump

entries.summary: A security hardening release: stricter validation and safer defaults across the retro server, remote tunnel, and external integrations, plus automated dependency and security scanning that keeps the project patched continuously.

highlights.text: Hardened retro board server and remote tunnel handling
Safer credential and config handling across integrations
Automated dependency updates and scheduled security scans

entries.summary: Analysis results got a full redesign: instead of one long wall of text you now land on an overview with section cards, each with an AI-written explanation of what the finding means for your team. Giving feedback when regenerating is also nicer with a proper multi-row text box.

highlights.text: Analysis results redesigned into an overview plus section cards with AI explanations
Regenerate-feedback prompt is now a large multi-row text box

entries.summary: A unified logging system: every mode now writes its own log file under ~/.yeaboi/logs, and you can change the log level live from the Settings page without restarting.

highlights.text: Per-mode log files for standup, retro, performance, reporting, and analysis
Live log-level control from the Settings page

entries.summary: Small visual fix: the animated wordmark intros for Standup and the setup wizard no longer show misaligned rows.

highlights.text: Fixed wordmark intro row misalignment on Standup and setup intros

entries.summary: Analysis polish and a safety net for ticket creation: the patterns page no longer crops its buttons, and generating tickets now asks for confirmation before anything is created.

highlights.text: Fixed button cropping on the analysis patterns page
Ticket generation now shows a confirmation gate before creating anything

entries.summary: Music player polish: the bar stays hidden during the startup splash and shows a connecting indicator while a stream buffers.

highlights.text: Music bar hidden during the startup splash
Connecting indicator while the music stream buffers

entries.summary: Confluence is now a first-class option in the Docs setup step, with its own standalone login so you can use it without configuring Jira.

highlights.text: Confluence selectable directly in the Docs setup step, no Jira required

entries.summary: Analysis reliability fixes: finished team profiles save to the right database, and each preview step is saved the moment it generates so nothing is lost mid-run.

highlights.text: Team profiles save to the active database instead of a legacy path
Each analysis preview step saves as soon as it generates

entries.summary: Scrolling across the app is now smooth — fast wheel bursts no longer stutter or tear the view.

highlights.text: Smooth scrolling with no view tearing across all pages

entries.summary: The voice-input install hint now shows the right command for how you installed yeaboi (uv, pipx, or source).

highlights.text: Voice install hint adapts to your install method

entries.summary: The big rebrand: scrum-agent is now yeaboi. The package renamed on PyPI (existing installs migrate via a shim), the repo moved to yeaboi.ai, and the project got its own landing page.

highlights.text: Package renamed scrum-agent → yeaboi on PyPI with a migration shim
New yeaboi.ai landing page

entries.summary: Setup wizard navigation upgrades: jump between sections with ←/→, press F to finish, and every connection field now shows a hint telling you where to get that token or URL.

highlights.text: ←/→ jump between wizard sections; F finishes setup
Where-to-get-it hints on every connection setup field

entries.summary: The setup wizard gained a Docs section so you can connect Notion or Confluence during first-time setup instead of editing environment variables by hand.

highlights.text: Docs setup step for Notion and Confluence in the wizard

entries.summary: Retro boards got social: teammates can react to cards with emojis, the join flow has a clear accent-colored share-code gate, and the in-app music player now has a real multi-channel library.

highlights.text: Emoji reactions on retro cards
Clearer share-code join gate for teammates
Expanded in-app music library

entries.summary: Music playback is more reliable: the backend switched to ffplay for headless playback and the app now notices when the audio daemon dies on its own.

highlights.text: Music backend switched to ffplay; dead-daemon detection

entries.summary: Animation consistency pass: planning and analysis page titles (including project-list and status screens) all shimmer like the rest of the app.

highlights.text: Shimmer animation on planning and analysis titles and status screens

entries.summary: Choosing an AI model is much nicer: the setup wizard discovers available models live from your provider, shows them as cards, and no longer offers retired models. The wizard itself got a visual polish with themed titles and threaded discovery so it never freezes.

highlights.text: Live model discovery with a card-based picker in the setup wizard
Retired models removed from the choices

entries.summary: Notion joined the toolbox: connect an integration token and yeaboi can search, read, and write Notion pages during analysis and planning, and include recent Notion activity in the daily standup.

highlights.text: Notion search/read/write tools for analysis and planning
Recent Notion pages feed the Daily Standup activity

entries.summary: Reporting mode arrived: generate a business-friendly delivery report of what shipped last sprint or last month, ready to send to stakeholders. The app also rebranded to yeaboi.ai with cinematic ANSI-shadow intros and a glinting splash wordmark.

highlights.text: New Reporting mode — stakeholder-ready delivery summaries
Rebrand to yeaboi.ai with cinematic per-mode intros

entries.summary: Performance mode arrived: per-engineer 1:1 prep, 1:1 completion with tracked action items, and a 6-month review builder — all fed by real Jira/Azure DevOps activity. You can also pick the exact LLM model in the setup wizard and settings.

highlights.text: New Performance mode — 1:1 prep, 1:1 completion, 6-month reviews
LLM model selection in setup wizard and settings

entries.summary: The planner got a lot smarter: choose an intake depth (Quick to Large), let the analyzer read your actual repository to inform the tech stack (including low-code detection), and past ceremony history now shapes new plans.

highlights.text: Selectable intake modes from Quick to Large
Repo-informed stack detection, including low-code platforms
Ceremony history (standups, retros) feeds new sprint plans

entries.summary: Retro mode arrived: host a collaborative sprint retrospective where teammates join from their browser on the LAN, add sticky cards to four grids, and let the AI propose action items from the feedback.

highlights.text: New Retro mode — collaborative LAN browser board with AI action items

entries.summary: Daily Standup mode arrived: yeaboi collects recent activity from your trackers and repos, scores sprint confidence, writes a summary, and delivers it to terminal, desktop, Slack, or email — with Markdown and HTML exports.

highlights.text: New Daily Standup mode with multi-channel delivery
Standup exports to Markdown and HTML

entries.summary: Quality-of-life release: an optional lofi music player embedded in the TUI (with an animated equalizer), rotating welcome-screen tips you can dismiss, and releases now version themselves automatically.

highlights.text: Embedded music player with channels and animated equalizer
Rotating, dismissible welcome-screen tips

entries.summary: Offline voice input landed — dictate answers instead of typing — plus graceful handling of API auth and billing errors, and fully automated PyPI releases on every version bump.

highlights.text: Offline voice input for answering questions
Graceful API auth/billing error handling

entries.summary: Analysis mode arrived: yeaboi studies your team's delivery history to build a team profile, then uses it to calibrate sprint plans to how your team actually works. The TUI also picked up assorted improvements.

highlights.text: New Analysis mode — team learning from delivery history
Calibrated planning informed by the team profile

entries.summary: Azure DevOps reached full parity with Jira — boards, velocity, and batch work-item creation — and the app gained opt-in anonymous telemetry.

highlights.text: Azure DevOps board parity with Jira
Opt-in anonymous telemetry

entries.summary: AWS Bedrock joined the provider list — run yeaboi on IAM credentials with no API key — alongside a full guide for deploying on AWS Lightsail.

highlights.text: AWS Bedrock provider (IAM credentials, no API key)
AWS Lightsail deployment guide

entries.summary: The first release of the AI Scrum Master: describe your project, answer a smart adaptive intake, and get epics, user stories, tasks, and sprint plans — reviewable step by step, exportable, and syncable to Jira in one batch. Includes a full-screen TUI and a headless CLI mode.

highlights.text: Smart adaptive intake with keyword extraction and follow-ups
Epics, stories, tasks, and sprint plans with human review checkpoints
One-shot Jira batch sync
Headless mode for CI and scripting
